steps.title: Isolate the radical
steps.content:
Subtract $27$ from both sides to get the square-root term by itself. This leaves $8$ on the right side.
$$\sqrt{w}=35-27=8$$

steps.title: Square both sides
steps.content:
Square both sides to undo the square root and solve for $w$.
$$w=8^2=64$$

steps.title: Check the value
steps.content: Substituting $64$ back into the original equation gives $\sqrt{64}+27=8+27=35$, so it satisfies the equation. The answer is 64.
